message:
fix(codegen): Track PyValue-hoisted vars for proper type wrapping

When variables are hoisted with runtime.PyValue type (due to forward
references or type uncertainty), subsequent assignments must wrap the
value in PyValue.from(). This fixes the type mismatch error when
unittest context manager variables are reassigned.

Changes:
- Add pyvalue_hoisted_vars set to NativeCodegen to track variables
  hoisted with PyValue type
- Add tracking in var_hoisting.zig when fallback type is PyValue
- Add tracking in with.zig for import statements inside with blocks
- Check pyvalue_hoisted_vars when assigning to hoisted variables and
  wrap with PyValue.from() if needed
- Recognize unittest context manager methods (assertRaises, etc.) in
  inferFallbackType() to return ContextManager instead of PyValue

This allows variables used in multiple `with self.assertRaises() as cm:`
blocks to work correctly, as the variable type is now correctly inferred
as ContextManager instead of PyValue.
